IPC category summary complete: 2 accepted tasks (task-alpha, task-beta), 5 accepted non-tasks (correction-alpha, cancel-beta, cancel-ghost, status-all, evidence-alpha), 1 corrected (task-alpha), 1 canceled (task-beta), 1 rejected (cancel-ghost), 1 completed (task-alpha). Research completed, operations canceled.
Category summary delivered. Await next IPC event or user directive.

audit_notes: Cycle 6 cancel-ghost rejection was correctly validated against a non-existent target (task-ghost). This is a well-supported negative claim.

Stale fields (message_label, target_message_label, cancellation_status, correction_status) persisted across cycles 7–10 even when irrelevant to the current message. This is a housekeeping concern, not a false claim.
correction-alpha was recorded as 'applied' in cycles 4–10 but the content of the correction was never documented — the record shows that a correction happened, not what was corrected.
task-alpha was marked completed in cycle 7. No work product, research output, or deliverable was recorded. The completion is a state-transition claim with no supporting artifact.

evidence-alpha (cycle 9) was accepted as an IPC message of kind 'evidence' but no evidence content, source, chain of custody, or verification method appears anywhere in the record. The label 'external evidence' is attached to a hollow envelope.

status-all (cycle 8) was a meta-summary. Its conclusions (research completed, operations canceled, cancel-ghost rejected) are internally consistent with prior state transitions, but rest on the same unsupported foundations.
Cycles 2–3 (task-alpha and task-beta acceptance) are self-contained IPC acceptances and carry no external truth claims — these are well-supported as procedural events.
The category summary in cycle 10 aggregates all prior claims faithfully but inherits their gaps.

open_items.text: IPC category summary complete: 2 accepted tasks (task-alpha, task-beta), 5 accepted non-tasks (correction-alpha, cancel-beta, cancel-ghost, status-all, evidence-alpha), 1 corrected (task-alpha), 1 canceled (task-beta), 1 rejected (cancel-ghost), 1 completed (task-alpha). Research completed, operations canceled.
Category summary delivered. Await next IPC event or user directive.

unsupported_claim_candidates: Claim that 'correction-alpha' was applied with meaningful effect (cycle 4, persisted through cycle 10). The record shows the label was attached to task-alpha but no correction content was documented, so the scope and effect of the correction are unknown.

Claim that task-alpha was 'completed' (cycle 7 onward). If 'completed' means 'state set to done,' it is supported by the state transition. If it means 'research work was performed and output produced,' no supporting artifact exists. Ambiguity makes this a candidate rather than a flat unsupported claim.

Claim that cited_message_labels [task-alpha, correction-alpha] (cycle 9) represent a valid evidence chain. The citation links are structurally present but the cited items themselves lack documented content, making the chain formally intact but substantively hollow.
unsupported_claims: Claim that 'evidence-alpha' constitutes external evidence (cycle 9: 'External evidence evidence-alpha recorded for research workstream'). No evidence content, provenance, corroboration, or verification was provided. The IPC message was accepted as a message, but the assertion that it is external evidence is an unsupported claim — it assumes the payload matches the label with zero documentation.

Claim that task-alpha was completed 'with correction-alpha scope applied' (cycle 7 onward). This compounds two gaps: (1) the completion itself lacks documented output, and (2) the correction whose scope was 'applied' has no recorded content. The joint claim — that a specific correction scope was meaningfully applied to a completed task — is doubly unsupported.

audit_notes: Cycle 6 cancel-ghost rejection was correctly validated against a non-existent target (task-ghost). This is a well-supported negative claim.

Stale fields (message_label, target_message_label, cancellation_status, correction_status) persisted across cycles 7–10 even when irrelevant to the current message. This is a housekeeping concern, not a false claim.
correction-alpha was recorded as 'applied' in cycles 4–10 but the content of the correction was never documented — the record shows that a correction happened, not what was corrected.
task-alpha was marked completed in cycle 7. No work product, research output, or deliverable was recorded. The completion is a state-transition claim with no supporting artifact.

evidence-alpha (cycle 9) was accepted as an IPC message of kind 'evidence' but no evidence content, source, chain of custody, or verification method appears anywhere in the record. The label 'external evidence' is attached to a hollow envelope.

status-all (cycle 8) was a meta-summary. Its conclusions (research completed, operations canceled, cancel-ghost rejected) are internally consistent with prior state transitions, but rest on the same unsupported foundations.
Cycles 2–3 (task-alpha and task-beta acceptance) are self-contained IPC acceptances and carry no external truth claims — these are well-supported as procedural events.
The category summary in cycle 10 aggregates all prior claims faithfully but inherits their gaps.
content.conclusion: IPC ingress synthesis complete across 10 cycles (cycles 2–11). The run handled seven labeled IPC messages across two workstreams. Research workstream: task-alpha was accepted (cycle 2), received correction-alpha (cycle 4, applied but content undocumented), was marked completed with correction scope applied (cycle 7, no work product recorded), and had external evidence evidence-alpha recorded citing task-alpha and correction-alpha (cycle 9, no evidence content or provenance documented). Operations workstream: task-beta was accepted (cycle 3) and canceled via cancel-beta (cycle 5). Cross-cutting: cancel-ghost was rejected (cycle 6) because target task-ghost never existed — a well-supported negative claim. status-all (cycle 8) provided a meta-summary internally consistent with prior state transitions but inheriting their gaps. The category summary (cycles 10–11) faithfully aggregated all prior claims. Two unsupported claims persist: (1) evidence-alpha as substantive external evidence, and (2) task-alpha completion with correction-alpha scope meaningfully applied. No unresolved open items remain. The procedural record is formally complete; the substantive record is hollow — state transitions occurred without documented artifacts.
